content: Just as the language learning definition of reflexion frames it as a process of reflecting on output to align internal understanding with external communication standards, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this structured pedagogical framework to ground their abstract self-correction in explicit, learning-oriented iterative feedback loops. This means LLM self-reflection can be improved by designing checklists modeled on high school writing reflexion exercises that ask models to critique their own outputs against clear, task-specific rubrics, similar to how students revise essays based on reflective writing prompts.
